meaning: n.报纸,政府的公报,(大学的)学报v.在公报上刊登,在公报上刊载
examples: The following is a letter that recently appeared in the Oak City Gazette, a local newspaper.
Bill Toland of the "Pittsburgh Post-Gazette" described both candidates' performances during the debate as "unstatesmanlike".
Sure, you can now get the Pittsburgh Post-Gazette in London or New York.
The Arkansas Democrat-Gazette is reprinting its 1957 front pages every day for three weeks.
For background on the parent-coach conflict, check out this March 2012 story from the Telegram-Gazette.
